question: A company sells a board game. According to the chart, which of the following statements is true about the most recent year shown?

options.A: The chart only shows results up to part of the most recent year, so it does not tell us how that full year will compare with earlier years.
options.B: The most recent year has clearly suffered a sharp drop in sales compared with all previous years.
options.C: Sales have declined every single year since the first year shown.
options.D: The most recent year must have the lowest annual sales in the entire period.

explanation: The latest year in the chart only includes about 5 months of data, while previous years show full 12‑month totals. Comparing the partial-year value (663 copies) directly to full-year values like 1,518 copies creates the illusion of a sharp decline. We cannot draw full‑year conclusions from this incomplete aggregate.
